title: Navigation History, Back Semantics, Snapshots, and Failure Recovery · Go Semantics Case: Pokedex Go History Commit
summary: Back recomputes offsets instead of restoring observed state, failed requests corrupt history, repeated pages grow memory forever, and concurrent prefetch mutates the visible cursor. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go history commit increment in bounded snapshot navigation history. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Push history only after a new view is admitted and leave cursor plus history unchanged on failure or cancellation.
Store immutable-enough page snapshots or identifiers with explicit copy boundaries so later mutation cannot rewrite history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
